title: Five near-identical *_level_count functions
description: Five private functions compute a decomposition-level count from a component slice. precomputed_level_count and precomputed_97_level_count are byte-for-byte identical (both read component.dwt.levels.len()); prequantized_97_level_count, preencoded_97_level_count and preencoded_compact_97_level_count are identical to each other (all read component.resolutions.len()-1) differing only in the input type and an error string.
evidence: Read of encode.rs:2416-2506 shows identical bodies. The three resolution-based variants differ only in the concrete component type and the 'prequantized' vs 'preencoded' word in one error message. Collapsible via a tiny trait (fn level_len(&self)->usize) implemented for the 5 component types, plus two generic fns.
reduction: Introduce one trait method returning the level length per component type and replace the 5 functions with 1-2 generic helpers.
verify_note: Verified encode.rs:2416-2505. precomputed_level_count and precomputed_97_level_count are byte-identical except the component type (both use dwt.levels.len()). prequantized/preencoded/preencoded_compact_97_level_count are identical to each other except the component type and the 'prequantized'/'preencoded' error word (all use resolutions.len().checked_sub(1)). So there are two small logic groups, both collapsible via a trait method (level_len) plus a generic helper; error-word/logic differences are parameterizable. Real, mergeable duplication. The 5 fns span ~90 lines; a trait impl (~13) + 1-2 generic helpers (~25) leaves ~38, i.e. ~50 saved rather than 60.

title: validate_*_htj2k97_image and validate_*_resolution copy-paste triplets
description: Three image-level validators and three resolution-level validators are near-identical. The image validators differ only in error strings and which resolution validator they call. The resolution validators share the same subband-count check, subband-order check, code-block-count/overflow checks and bitplane-count formula (guard_bits.saturating_add(exponent).saturating_sub(1)); they diverge only in the final per-code-block payload check (validate_band_len vs validate_preencoded_code_block_payload vs validate_preencoded_compact_code_block_payload).
evidence: Read of encode.rs:2507-2830: validate_prequantized_resolution / validate_preencoded_resolution / validate_preencoded_compact_resolution bodies are identical except the trailing per-block validation call and 'prequantized'/'preencoded' error words. Same for the three *_image wrappers.
reduction: Extract one generic resolution validator parameterized by a per-code-block closure/callback, and one generic image validator that drives it; keep the payload check as the only per-variant piece.
verify_note: Verified encode.rs:2507-2830. validate_prequantized/preencoded/preencoded_compact_resolution have identical control flow (subband count/order checks, expected_blocks overflow, empty-subband check, bitplane count, code-block count) differing only in: the per-block validation call (validate_band_len vs validate_preencoded_code_block_payload vs validate_preencoded_compact_code_block_payload), the compact variant's extra payload_len parameter, and the 'prequantized'/'preencoded' error words. The three *_image wrappers are likewise identical except the same error word and the compact payload_len threading. Genuinely mergeable via a generic validator + per-variant closure, but abstracting subband/code-block field access across three distinct concrete types requires trait boilerplate, so net savings are ~110 rather than 130.

title: prepared_resolution_packets/prepared_subband per-variant builder family
description: Four prepared_resolution_packets_from_* functions have identical structure (enumerate resolutions, build PreparedResolutionPacket with component/resolution/precinct=0, map subbands) differing only in iter vs into_iter and the subband mapper. The prepared_subband_from_* builders all populate the same PreparedEncodeSubband fields (num_cbs_x/y, code_block_width/height via max, width/height via precomputed_subband_width/height, sub_band_type, total_bitplanes, block_coding_mode=HighThroughput, ht_target_coding_passes=1) with only the coefficients/preencoded_ht_code_blocks source differing.
evidence: Read of encode.rs:2832-3112 shows prepared_subband_from_prequantized vs prepared_subband_from_preencoded vs prepared_subband_from_preencoded_owned build the same struct with the same width/height/max boilerplate repeated verbatim.
reduction: Factor the common PreparedResolutionPacket assembly and the common PreparedEncodeSubband field computation into generic helpers taking a per-variant closure that yields (coefficients, preencoded_ht_code_blocks).
verify_note: Verified encode.rs:2832-3135. prepared_subband_from_prequantized/preencoded/preencoded_owned build the same PreparedEncodeSubband with verbatim-repeated code_block_width/height (max), width/height (precomputed_subband_width/height), sub_band_type, total_bitplanes, block_coding_mode, ht_target_coding_passes boilerplate; only the coefficients/preencoded_ht_code_blocks payload differs. Those, plus the borrow resolution-packet builders (prequantized vs preencoded, identical except the subband mapper), are mergeable. However the compact variants (prepared_resolution_packets_from_preencoded_compact_component and prepared_subband_from_preencoded_compact) return DIFFERENT types (PreparedCompactResolutionPacket/PreparedCompactSubband with different fields) and are NOT mergeable with the others, and the owned variant uses into_iter/unzip (partially divergent). Real duplication but scoped to 3 of the listed items; 120 is inflated, realistic ~70.

title: Unused i32 wrapper encode_code_block_with_style (already tagged #[allow(dead_code)])
description: pub(crate) fn encode_code_block_with_style is an i32->i64 wrapper that is never called; the author already flagged it with #[allow(dead_code)]. Its i64 counterpart and the segments variants are used, but this specific wrapper is not.
evidence: rg across crates/: `encode_code_block_with_style\b` (excluding _i64) matches only its own definition at bitplane_encode.rs:328. Contrast the sibling encode_code_block_segments_with_style which is called from lib.rs:845.
reduction: Delete the function and its #[allow(dead_code)] attribute.
verify_note: rg across crates/ and xtask/ for the exact name (word boundary, excluding _i64) yields only the definition at bitplane_encode.rs:328; the other hits (287,337,347,521) are the _i64 variant. It is pub(crate), so it cannot be part of any crate's external public API and cannot be re-exported beyond j2k-native; no macro/trait/FFI/build.rs usage. Sibling encode_code_block_segments_with_style is genuinely called, confirming this one is uniquely orphaned. Truly dead; deleting the 19 lines (327-345) plus the #[allow(dead_code)] is safe.

title: Oversized public encode entry-point wrapper matrix
description: The precomputed/prequantized/preencoded 9/7+5/3 surface exposes a combinatorial matrix of thin public wrappers (base, _with_accelerator, _with_mct, _with_mct_and_accelerator) that all funnel into one private impl. Several intermediates have no non-test consumer anywhere in the workspace and exist only to fill out the matrix, inflating the deny(missing_docs) public surface.
evidence: rg -rln (excluding encode.rs tests): encode_precomputed_j2k_53_with_accelerator, _with_mct, _with_mct_and_accelerator, and encode_precomputed_htj2k_53_with_mct_and_accelerator appear ONLY in the lib.rs re-export list; the actual external users are encode_precomputed_htj2k_53_with_mct (j2k/src/recode.rs) and the base/owned/compact forms (j2k-transcode). Each unused wrapper is ~7 lines + a doc block.
reduction: Keep the fully-parameterized *_with_mct_and_accelerator (or a single builder-style options arg) and the couple of base wrappers actually consumed; remove the intermediate permutations with no external caller.

title: encode.rs god-file (10.8k lines) mixing unrelated encode entry-point families
location: crates/j2k-native/src/j2c/encode.rs (10815 lines; non-test through line 8512, tests 8513-10815)
description: A single module holds the classic-vs-HT, reversible-vs-irreversible, single-vs-multi-tile, and precomputed/prequantized/preencoded/compact entry-point families plus their validators, prepared builders, DwtPlanes trait glue, and ~2300 lines of tests. This concentration is what enables the per-variant copy-paste reported above and makes review/navigation hard.
evidence: wc -l = 10815; rg shows ~90 fn definitions before the test module at 8513 and 54 #[test] functions. The precomputed/prequantized/preencoded families (level_count/validate/prepared/entry-point) alone span ~1300-3160.
reduction: Split into submodules e.g. encode/mod.rs (core encode_impl + options), encode/precomputed.rs, encode/prequantized.rs, encode/preencoded.rs, encode/validate.rs; consolidating the duplicated helpers during the move compounds the LOC savings.
verify_note: wc -l confirms 10815 lines with the test module starting at 8513, and the file mixes precomputed/prequantized/preencoded encode entry-point families plus their level_count/validate/prepared helpers - a genuine maintainability defect, and splitting into submodules is a safe, sound reorganization. However, the est_loc_saved is misattributed: moving code into submodules relocates rather than removes lines (it actually adds mod declarations, net ~0), and the finding's own evidence states the 150 comes from 'consolidating the duplicated helpers during the move', which is exactly findings 1-3 - pure double counting. As a standalone code-reduction the net LOC saved is ~0.

title: Clippy too_many_arguments cluster and stringly-typed errors in hot encode paths
description: 18 functions across encode.rs/ht_block_encode.rs suppress too_many_arguments rather than grouping parameters into a context/options struct, and encode.rs threads Result<_, &'static str> through 200+ signatures. The arg lists (and repeated width/height/num_components/guard_bits/step_sizes tuples) are exactly what makes the prepared_* and validate_* helpers verbose.
evidence: rg -c too_many_arguments: encode.rs=7, ht_block_encode.rs=11. rg -c "&'static str" encode.rs=212.
reduction: Introduce a small EncodeCtx/SubbandGeometry struct to carry the repeated width/height/level/guard/step tuple, removing most allow() suppressions and shrinking signatures; optionally a single error enum.

title: compute.rs is a 17.7k-LOC god-file (273 fns, 42 structs) despite 22 existing submodules
description: compute.rs already declares 22 child modules (mod abi, direct_*, classic_*, resident_*, etc.) yet retains 273 functions and 42 structs inline, mixing runtime setup, prepared-plan construction, IDWT/MCT/HT/classic dispatch, resident packet planning, profiling, and ~800 LOC of test-only parity encoders in one file. This defeats the compiler's per-item dead-code detection at the module boundary, makes review/navigation impractical, and is the root reason duplicate helpers (below) accumulate unnoticed.
evidence: wc -l src/compute.rs = 17757; grep counts: 273 `fn`, 42 `struct`, 8 `impl`; 22 `mod` declarations at top of file; test-only parity encoders (encode_classic_tier1_code_blocks_via_*_for_test) occupy 12603-13500 inside the production file though correctly #[cfg(all(test,target_os="macos"))].
reduction: Continue the existing extraction pattern: move the classic-encode, HT-encode, IDWT/MCT prep, resident packet-planning, and prepared-plan cropping clusters into the already-present compute/ submodule tree (or new ones), and relocate the #[cfg(test)] parity encoders into compute/tests.rs. Splitting re-enables private dead-code warnings per module.
verify_note: The core defect is real and verified: wc-equivalent line count is 17757, and there are ~25 mod declarations already at the top of the file (abi, classic_encode_pipeline, direct_*, resident_*, etc.), confirming an established extraction pattern that continued splitting would follow safely. The specific counts in the title are inflated (I measured 217 fn definitions and 28 top-level structs, not 273/42), but the god-file conclusion stands. The reduction (extract clusters into the existing compute/ submodule tree, relocate #[cfg(test)] parity encoders) is safe and sound. However the est_loc_saved of 150 is not a genuine code reduction: splitting relocates code (net ~0 LOC, likely slightly higher due to module/use boilerplate); the '150 saved' relies on speculative dead-code being surfaced by per-module privacy warnings, which is not demonstrated. Confirmed as an architecture/maintainability improvement, but corrected the LOC-reduction estimate to ~0.

title: Copy-pasted next_label if/else waterfall repeated ~7 times in the encode driver
description: The resident-encode driver computes a `next_label` string via a near-identical if-else chain (profile_classic_tier1_raw_pack ? ... : profile_classic_tier1_arithmetic_pack ? ... : ... : "j2k classic resident packetization") before each stage's finish_resident_encode_split_command_buffer_timed call. The chain is duplicated ~7 times, each time dropping one leading branch, so the same ordered label list is transcribed repeatedly.
evidence: "j2k classic resident packetization" literal appears 11x and "...Tier-1 raw-pack profile" 4x in compute.rs; finish_resident_encode_split_command_buffer_timed called 17x. The blocks at 16405, 16455, 16487, 16523, 16558, 16590, 16622, 16653 are structurally identical label ladders differing only by which profile flags remain.
reduction: Replace with a single ordered slice of (flag, label) pairs and a helper that returns the first enabled label at/after a given stage index; call it at each stage. Collapses ~130 lines to ~20.
verify_note: Verified 9 `let next_label = if ...` ladders in the driver (16338, 16404, 16453, 16491, 16528, 16563, 16595, 16627, 16659), not ~7 — the count is if anything understated. Three (16338/16404/16453) are identical full ladders (density->packetization) and the rest are exact suffixes of the same ordered list, each returning the first enabled profile_classic_tier1_* label at/after a stage, defaulting to 'j2k classic resident packetization'. This is genuinely the 'first-enabled-label-from-stage-index over an ordered (flag,label) slice' pattern the finding proposes; consolidation is behavior-preserving and metal-only (no cross-backend perf/behavior concern). Real, mergeable duplication. Line count: ~95 lines of ladders replaced by ~12-line helper + ~9 call sites (~21 lines), so net saving is closer to ~75 than the claimed 90.

title: Broad cross-product of near-identical public decode methods each with a duplicated non-macOS stub
location: src/lib.rs:927-1200 (decode_*_to_device_with_report / decode_*_to_host_surface / decode_*_to_cpu_staged_metal_surface_with_session / decode_*_to_device_with_session families), stubs at :1012,1084,1121,1153,1192 etc.
description: The public surface repeats a Full/Region/Scaled/RegionScaled cross product for several output kinds; the *_with_report and *_to_host_surface variants are 4-way copies differing only in operation enum and inner call, and the *_cpu_staged_metal_surface_with_session / *_to_device_with_session variants each carry a hand-written `#[cfg(not(target_os="macos"))] { let _ = (..); Err(Error::MetalUnavailable) }` stub duplicated per method.
evidence: 20 `pub fn decode_*` methods in lib.rs (grep list 853-1946); 11 identical `Err(Error::MetalUnavailable)` non-macOS stubs (all in lib.rs) each preceded by a `let _ = (...)`; the four decode_*_to_cpu_staged_metal_surface_with_session bodies (1065-1200) differ only in the DeviceDecodeRequest variant and the decode_*_into call.
reduction: Fold the four staged-upload variants into one helper taking a DeviceDecodeRequest; generate the *_with_report wrappers from a macro over (Operation, inner-fn); and introduce a `macos_only!`/`metal_unavailable_stub!` macro to eliminate the repeated cfg(not(macos)) stub bodies.

title: ~800 LOC of test-only parity encoders embedded mid-file in production compute.rs
description: Six #[cfg(all(test,target_os="macos"))] parity helpers that each re-run the classic Tier-1 encode via a different token-pack route solely so encode/tests.rs can assert cross-route equivalence. They are correctly test-gated (not shipped) but bloat the production file and overlap heavily in batch-job/buffer setup with the real encode_classic_tier1_prepared_device_code_blocks path.
evidence: Each defined under #[cfg(all(test,target_os="macos"))] (compute.rs:12602,12769,12778,12787,12962,13382); consumed only from src/encode/tests.rs:4265-4271. Bodies duplicate the coefficient/batch_jobs/output_capacity assembly loop seen in the production encoder.
reduction: Move these helpers into compute/tests.rs (or a test submodule) and factor their shared batch-job/buffer-setup loop into one helper reused by the real encoder, so tests exercise the production assembly path instead of re-implementing it.

title: Combinatorial explosion of thin decode_tile* free-function wrappers
description: 15 public free functions form a (region? x scaled? x in_context? x with_options?) matrix where every non-terminal variant is pure delegation (fills DecodeOptions::default() or forwards to a JpegBatchSession one-shot). This is a large public surface that could collapse to a few entry points taking an options/params struct.
evidence: decode_tile_into (1831) just wraps decode_tile_into_in_context via DEFAULT_CONTEXT (0 external callers); decode_tile_region_into_in_context_with_options (2054) has 0 external callers; the 8 non-with_options variants each only insert DecodeOptions::default() then call their _with_options twin (e.g. 1846-1863, 1923-1929, 1962-1968, 1999-2005, 2029-2048, 2071-2090, 2114-2135). Bodies are 2-12 lines of delegation each.
reduction: Collapse each family to one function taking a small params/options struct (roi: Option<Rect>, scale: Downscale, options: DecodeOptions), dropping the default-filling twins and the 0-use variants; keep 4-6 entry points instead of 15.

title: progressive12 and extended12 region-decode dispatch scaffolds are near-identical
location: crates/j2k-jpeg/src/decoder.rs:3397-3534 (decode_progressive12_region_into) vs 3797-3943 (decode_extended12_region_into), plus their color444/subsampled/four_component siblings (3536-3718 vs 3945-4260)
description: The 12-bit region decoders duplicate the same dispatch preamble (SofKind guard, roi.is_within bounds check emitting RectOutOfBounds, the Rgb16 colorspace match routing Rgb/YCbCr to color444 vs color_subsampled and Cmyk/Ycck to four_component, then the grayscale single-component fallback). Only the coefficient source differs (progressive_plan + dequantize_progressive12_block vs a BitReader running decode_extended12_block_pixels). The whole progressive12/extended12 family runs ~880 LOC of largely parallel code.
evidence: Lines 3411-3491 (progressive12) and 3805-3884 (extended12) are structurally identical apart from progressive_color_sampling vs extended12_color_sampling and self.progressive_plan vs self.plan; both share Extended12Output, Extended12RgbProjection, Extended12ColorSampling, write_extended12_block_region and the same RectOutOfBounds/NotImplemented error shapes.
reduction: Factor the shared dispatch/guards into one generic region driver parameterized over a coefficient-source closure/trait (progressive plan vs sequential bit reader), leaving only the per-mode block producers.
verify_note: There is genuine surface similarity in the top-level dispatch/guard shells (decoder.rs:3397-3491 vs 3797-3884): both do a SofKind check, the same roi.is_within/RectOutOfBounds guard, an Rgb16 color_space match, and the same grayscale-fallthrough NotImplemented check, and both share Extended12Output/Extended12RgbProjection/Extended12ColorSampling and the write_extended12_* helpers. But the evidence's 'structurally identical apart from sampling/plan' claim overstates it: (1) the preambles differ (progressive first does self.progressive_plan.as_ref().ok_or(...)? plus a combined sof_kind+color_space check; extended checks sof_kind only), and (2) the Cmyk/Ycck arm differs structurally (progressive calls a single decode_progressive12_four_component_region_into; extended branches into 444 vs subsampled). More importantly, the bulk cited as mergeable siblings (3492-3718 vs 3885-4260) is NOT safely mergeable: progressive sources coefficients from precomputed decode_progressive_dct_blocks with block_rows/block_cols indexing and no inline restart handling, whereas extended runs a sequential BitReader over scan bytes with per-MCU decode_extended12_block_pixels, inline consume_extended12_restart restart logic, and finish_scan. These are fundamentally different entropy-decode algorithms with different iteration order (single-component block scan vs interleaved-MCU scan). A generic driver would need a 5-6-method trait to abstract them, adding infrastructure that offsets much of the win. Realistic safe reduction is limited to the dispatch shell plus the subsampled write tail (~40-60 LOC), not 180.

title: JPEG zig-zag table defined verbatim twice; second copy over-broadly pub and unused externally
description: Two identical [u8; 64] standard JPEG zig-zag scan tables coexist: pub(crate) ZIGZAG and pub JPEG_BASELINE_ZIGZAG. The latter is re-exported from adapter/mod.rs but not referenced by any other crate.
evidence: Byte-for-byte identical contents (0,1,8,16,9,2,3,10,...,62,63). rg for JPEG_BASELINE_ZIGZAG outside crates/j2k-jpeg returns nothing; internal users are only encoder.rs:755 and adapter code.
reduction: Keep one pub(crate) const (entropy::ZIGZAG), point encoder.rs/adapter at it, delete JPEG_BASELINE_ZIGZAG and its re-export.
verify_note: Verified byte-for-byte identical contents: entropy/mod.rs:14 ZIGZAG (pub(crate) [u8;64]) and adapter/baseline_encode.rs:225 JPEG_BASELINE_ZIGZAG (pub [u8;64]) both contain 0,1,8,16,9,2,3,10,...,62,63. Internal-only usage is confirmed: JPEG_BASELINE_ZIGZAG is used at encoder.rs:755 and baseline_encode.rs:304, and rg finds no consumer outside crates/j2k-jpeg (the CUDA/Metal copies are separate GPU-language constants, not references). The values are semantically identical so consolidation cannot change behavior. Caveat: JPEG_BASELINE_ZIGZAG is re-exported via `pub use` from the `pub mod adapter` (adapter/mod.rs:21), so it is public API; the safe consolidation keeps a pub re-export of the single const rather than outright deleting the name to avoid a breaking API change. The est_loc_saved of 12 is inflated -- deleting the duplicate array (baseline_encode.rs:225-229) plus its doc comment is ~6 lines; net saving after wiring imports is ~8 LOC.

title: Backend dispatch match arms are identical across all BackendKind variants
description: Several dispatch methods match on self.kind but every arm calls the same scalar impl (RGBA fills have no SIMD backend) or returns the same literal (prefers_cropped_420_region returns true for all kinds). The match adds no dispatch value.
evidence: fill_rgba_row_from_gray Avx2/Neon arms both call scalar::fill_rgba_row_from_gray (108-111); fill_rgba_row_from_rgb/ycbcr likewise; prefers_cropped_420_region returns true in Scalar, Avx2 and Neon arms (222-227).
reduction: Replace these matches with a single direct scalar call / single `true`, dropping the per-kind arms (clippy match_same_arms is currently allowed, which hides this).

title: decoder.rs is a 7422-line god-file with one ~3900-line impl block
description: A single impl block mixes parse/plan-building, baseline decode, and three large orthogonal codec families (lossless: 2359-3377, progressive12/extended12: 3377-4260) plus trait impls. The crate already has a src/lossless/ module (only holding predict helpers) and Extended12* helper types, so the seams exist; the decode methods just were not moved out.
evidence: rg '^impl ' shows the block spans 2173-6051; rg '^    fn ' lists ~90 methods in it, of which the decode_lossless_*/decode_progressive12_*/decode_extended12_* families alone are ~50 methods over ~1900 lines.
reduction: Split decode_lossless_* into src/lossless/decode.rs and the 12-bit families into src/decoder/extended12.rs / progressive12.rs; this shrinks the reviewable file and is a prerequisite for the dedup in the progressive12/extended12 finding.

title: 10-arm `any(feature = "cuda-oxide-*")` cfg list hand-repeated ~28 times
location: context.rs (14x, e.g. lines 21-32, 51-62, 424-435, 476-487), kernels.rs (4x, e.g. 256-268), build_flags.rs (4x, e.g. 3-14, 33-44), execution.rs (3x), driver.rs (2x, lines 116-127, 133-144)
description: The identical 10-feature list (`cuda-oxide-copy-u8`, `-j2k-encode`, `-j2k-decode-store`, `-j2k-dequantize`, `-j2k-idwt`, `-htj2k-decode`, `-htj2k-encode`, `-transcode`, `-jpeg-decode`, `-jpeg-encode`) is spelled out inside `#[cfg(any(...))]` / `#[cfg_attr(not(any(...)), allow(dead_code))]` attributes ~28 separate times, each spanning ~10-13 source lines. Adding/removing a kernel family requires editing all ~28 sites.
evidence: `rg -c 'feature = "cuda-oxide-copy-u8"'` (first entry of each block) reports 28 occurrences: context.rs:14, kernels.rs:4, build_flags.rs:4, execution.rs:3, driver.rs:2, tests.rs:1. No aggregate feature exists in Cargo.toml (features are all independent).
reduction: Add one internal aggregate feature (e.g. `_cuda-oxide = []`) and make each `cuda-oxide-*` feature imply it (`cuda-oxide-copy-u8 = ["_cuda-oxide"]`, etc.). Replace every 10-arm `any(...)` block with a single `#[cfg(feature = "_cuda-oxide")]` (and `not(feature="_cuda-oxide")` for the dead_code allows). Collapses ~10 attribute lines per site to 1.
verify_note: Verified 28 blocks of the identical 10-arm feature list (context.rs 14, kernels.rs 4, build_flags.rs 4, execution.rs 3, driver.rs 2, tests.rs 1), appearing in both `#[cfg(any(...))]` and `#[cfg_attr(not(any(...)), allow(dead_code))]` forms. Cargo.toml declares all cuda-oxide-* features as independent `= []` with no aggregate, so the proposed internal `_cuda-oxide` shim (each feature implying it) is valid and collapses each ~12-14 line block to one line. Estimate of 250 is reasonable and if anything conservative (actual ~300-350 gross minus ~11 Cargo.toml lines).

title: Per-kernel-family PTX accessor/guard boilerplate repeated 10x across 3 files (macro-able)
location: build_flags.rs:64-235 (10x ensure_*_ptx_built + 10x CUDA_OXIDE_*_PTX_BUILT const), kernels.rs:375-404 & 562-610 (10x PTX const + 10x accessor fn), context.rs:125-383 (10x cuda_oxide_*_kernel_function feature+fallback pairs) & 1147-1165 (CompiledKernelKey::ptx match)
description: For each of the 10 kernel families there is a near-identical set of items differing only by the family name/feature string: an `ensure_<fam>_ptx_built()` fn, a `CUDA_OXIDE_<FAM>_PTX_BUILT` const, an `include_bytes!` PTX const, a `cuda_oxide_<fam>_ptx()` accessor, a feature-gated `cuda_oxide_<fam>_kernel_function()` plus a `not(feature)` fallback returning `cuda_oxide_feature_missing(...)`, and a `CompiledKernelKey::CudaOxide<Fam>` PTX-mapping arm. This is ~500 lines of stamped-out repetition.
evidence: build_flags.rs shows 10 copies of the `ensure_*` fn body (only the family/feature string differs) and 10 copies of the `*_PTX_BUILT` const. context.rs:125-383 shows the 10 kernel_function methods each with a feature + not-feature variant of identical shape (only label strings + `is_*_stage()` check + KernelKey variant differ). kernels.rs:375-404 and 562-610 show 10 include_bytes consts + 10 trivial accessors.
reduction: Drive all of it from one declarative macro invoked with a table of `(feature-lit, FamilyIdent, KeyVariant, is_*_stage, human-label, ptx-file-stem)` rows, generating the const, accessor, ensure_* guard, kernel_function pair, and ptx match arm per row. Would reduce the ~500 lines to a ~40-line macro plus a ~30-line table.
verify_note: Confirmed 10x `ensure_*_ptx_built` fns (build_flags.rs:64-192) + 10x `*_PTX_BUILT` consts (193-222), 10x `include_bytes!` PTX consts (kernels.rs:375-404) + 10x trivial accessor fns (562-610), and 10x `cuda_oxide_*_kernel_function` feature/fallback pairs (context.rs:125-383). Each family differs only in feature literal, const/KernelKey name, `is_*_stage()` check, and label string, making them drivable from one declarative macro + table. copy_u8 is a minor special case (no kernel arg / stage check) the macro can accommodate. ~500 lines reducible to a small macro+table; estimate of 300 is sound and conservative.

title: bytes.rs: ~45 one-line wrappers that just forward to GpuAbi::as_bytes/slice_as_bytes
description: The module defines ~45 named `pub(crate)` functions such as `store_gray8_job_as_bytes`, `inverse_mct_job_as_bytes`, `htj2k_dequantize_jobs_as_bytes`, `cuda_jpeg_huffman_table_as_bytes`, each whose entire body is `<T as GpuAbi>::as_bytes(x)` or `::slice_as_bytes(x)`. They add a per-type name for a fully generic operation, and several are called from only 1-2 sites.
evidence: `rg -c 'as GpuAbi>::(as_bytes|slice_as_bytes)' bytes.rs` = 45. Call-site check: `store_gray8_job_as_bytes`/`inverse_mct_job_as_bytes`/`store_rgb16_mct_job_as_bytes` each 1 def + 2 uses in j2k_decode.rs; `htj2k_dequantize_jobs_as_bytes` 1 def + 2 uses; the many `cuda_jpeg_*`/`htj2k_packetization_*` wrappers are thin single-purpose forwards.
reduction: Replace the family of wrappers with two generic free fns (`fn as_bytes<T: GpuAbi>(&T) -> &[u8]`, `fn slice_as_bytes<T: GpuAbi>(&[T]) -> &[u8]`, plus `_mut` variants) or call `<T as GpuAbi>::as_bytes` directly at the handful of call sites. Keeps only the non-trivial helpers (byte-len checks like `htj2k_statuses_byte_len`).

title: tests.rs is a 4.1k-line single-module god-file
description: All unit/integration tests for context, memory, execution, j2k/htj2k/jpeg decode+encode, packetize and transcode live in one `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` file, making it hard to review and encouraging copy-paste test scaffolding. This is the largest file in the crate.
evidence: wc -l ranks tests.rs at 4142 lines, ~2x the next file (j2k_decode.rs 2122). It is a single module included via `#[cfg(test)] mod tests;` in lib.rs:29-30.
reduction: Split into per-area test modules (or move to a `tests/` directory), and factor repeated fixture/setup helpers into a shared test-support module. Primarily improves reviewability; modest net LOC savings from deduplicated scaffolding.

title:
fast444 path is a hand-copied parallel of the generic FastSubsampledMetal

path
location: crates/j2k-jpeg-metal/src/compute.rs:2370-2799 (encode_fast444_* batch items), 4356-5875 (try_decode_fast444_* batch surfaces/textures), 7736-8227 (try_decode_fast444_* single-surface), 1443-1524 (fast444_*params); vs generic 1788-2367,3272-3695,7282-7665
description:
4:2:0 and 4:2:2 share one generic implementation via the `FastSubsampledMetal`/`FastSubsampledPacket` traits and `

`-generic functions, but 4:4:4 has a completely separate concrete function set (encode_fast444_batch_item, try_decode_fast444_full_rgb_batch_to_surfaces, ...region_scaled..., etc.) that mirrors the generic scaffolding almost line-for-line. Compare encode_fast444_batch_item (compute.rs:2709) with encode_fast_subsampled_batch_item

(compute.rs:2220): identical plane allocation, entropy_decode_thread_count, status/entropy/restart/checkpoint buffers, dc/ac PreparedHuffmanHost arrays, decode encoder dispatch, and BatchedDecodeItem construction; the only real deltas are field-vs-method access (packet.dimensions vs packet.dimensions()), a PlaneMode param, and use of encode_jpeg_pack_to_surface_in_command_buffer for the pack step. JpegFast444PacketV1 already implements the unifying FastPacketAccess trait (impl_fast_packet_access! at compute.rs:725), so the field/method gap is already bridged.
evidence: By-name LOC sum: `*fast444*` functions total 2494 LOC vs 2997 LOC for `*fast_subsampled*` generic functions (python span sum over compute.rs). 29 fast444 functions enumerated via rg. region_scaled_plan.rs additionally carries a Fast444RegionScaledGroup struct (line 274) paralleling FastRegionScaledGroup (line 160). Dispatchers (decode_full_batch_to_surfaces_with_runtime, compute.rs:7120) call the generic path with ::<JpegFast420PacketV1>/::<JpegFast422PacketV1> then a separate fast444 branch.
reduction:
Extend FastSubsampledMetal (or add a FastPackedMetal trait) so JpegFast444PacketV1 implements it, carrying the PlaneMode/pack-step differences via an associated const or a `pack_to_surface` hook, then delete the concrete fast444 batch-item and try_decode_fast444_* families and route 444 through the generic ::

functions.
verify_note:
Duplication is real: JpegFast444PacketV1 has no impl FastSubsampledMetal (only 420/422 do), and 29 concrete fast444_* functions structurally mirror the generic ::

functions (verified 4380 vs 3303: identical empty/op guard, from_batched loop, restart/checkpoint check, groups+grouped delegation, thread-count computation). region_scaled_plan.rs also carries a parallel Fast444RegionScaledGroup (274) vs FastRegionScaledGroup (160). However the est is inflated. The 444 path carries a genuine extra dimension the subsampled trait has no concept of: a runtime PlaneMode (Gray/YCbCr/Rgb) that changes color-space handling in the pack step and is keyed into grouping; it reuses the scaled-region kernel (fast444_scaled_region_batch_decode_pipeline) with different param structs (JpegFastRegionScaledBatchParams / JpegFast444ScaledParams) instead of dedicated full kernels; it uses chroma_width=chroma_height=full (non-subsampled) whereas the generic path hardcodes width.div_ceil(2)/P::chroma_height; and it lacks the timing/split-coeff-debug machinery. A trait extension (as proposed) can dedup the skeleton, but the divergent PlaneMode-aware pack, kernel selection, and non-subsampled chroma still need code, so net savings are closer to ~700 than 1200.

title: RGB-surface and RGBA-texture batch decoders duplicate large shared prefixes
location: crates/j2k-jpeg-metal/src/compute.rs:3303-3695 (full_rgb..surfaces) vs 3789-4288 (full_rgba..textures); repeated for region_scaled at 5998-6226 vs 6444-6661, and per-444 twins at 4380-4677 vs 4677-4943, 5076-5355 vs 5550-5800
description: For every family/op there is a `_rgb_batch_to_surfaces_*` function and a near-identical `_rgba_batch_to_textures_*` function. The two share the entire front half: emptiness/op/fmt guard, P::from_batched packet collection, restart/checkpoint eligibility check, fast_subsampled_full_rgb_batch_groups grouping + multi-group delegation, and checked_u32 tile/segment/thread-count math. They diverge only at the output-binding tail (surface buffer vs MetalBatchTextureOutput/MetalTextureTile).
evidence: try_decode_fast_subsampled_full_rgb_batch_to_surfaces_with_mode_and_output (3303) and try_decode_fast_subsampled_full_rgba_batch_to_textures (3789) both begin with the same empty/op guard, `for packet in packets { P::from_batched... }`, the same `(!P::FULL_RGB_BATCH_SUPPORTS_RESTART && first.restart_interval_mcus()!=0) || first.entropy_checkpoints().is_empty()` check, and the same fast_subsampled_full_rgb_batch_groups+grouped delegation (verified by reading both). By-name LOC: `*rgba*texture*` functions = 1855 LOC.
reduction: Extract the shared validation+grouping+thread-count computation into one helper returning a prepared plan, then have the surface and texture variants only implement the divergent output binding.
verify_note: Verified 3303 (surfaces) and 3789 (textures) share an identical prefix: empty/op guard, for-packet from_batched loop, the (!FULL_RGB_BATCH_SUPPORTS_RESTART && restart!=0)||checkpoints.is_empty() check, fast_subsampled_full_rgb_batch_groups + grouped delegation, and the width/height/chroma/y_len/chroma_len/JpegFast420BatchParams computation (~100 LOC). That prefix is genuinely extractable without behavior change. But 500 is inflated: the shared prefix is only ~100 LOC per pair, the entropy handling already diverges (surfaces builds entropy vectors inline while textures uses batch_entropy_buffers), surfaces carries FastBatchTiming instrumentation textures lacks, and a 'prepared plan' helper adds a return struct plus call boilerplate. Realistic net saving across the surface/texture pairs is ~250.

title: lib.rs is a god-file with ~4510 lines of inline tests (61% of the file)
description: lib.rs is 7352 lines; the inline `mod tests` starts at line 2842 and runs to EOF, i.e. ~4510 lines / 83 test functions are embedded in the crate root, dwarfing the ~2840 lines of actual production code. This is inconsistent with compute.rs, which externalizes its tests via `mod tests;` -> compute/tests.rs (compute.rs:8501). The huge inline block makes the production API surface of the crate root hard to navigate and review.
evidence: wc -l lib.rs = 7352; `mod tests {` at line 2842 with matching closing brace at file end (7352); rg counted 83 `#[test]`/test fns. compute.rs uses `mod tests;` (line 8501) pointing at the separate 1619-line compute/tests.rs.
reduction: Move lib.rs::tests into a `mod tests;` external file (e.g. src/tests.rs) mirroring compute.rs; shrinks the reviewable root by ~4500 lines (relocation, not net deletion).
verify_note: Verified: #[cfg(test)] mod tests { at line 2842, file ends at 7352 (~4510 test lines, 61%), 83 test fns/#[test] counted. compute.rs already uses the external `mod tests;` pattern (line 8501) pointing at compute/tests.rs, so moving lib.rs tests to src/tests.rs is a safe, precedented relocation. The claim is accurate. Note the finding itself concedes this is relocation, not deletion: net LOC removed from the workspace is ~0 (the ~4500 lines move to another file). The value is reviewability of the crate root, not code reduction, so the loc-saved metric should be 0.

title: Three separate Option<&JpegFastNPacketV1> params threaded through ~80 sites each
description: The fast 4:4:4/4:2:2/4:2:0 packet options are passed as three parallel `Option<&...>` arguments (fast444_packet, fast422_packet, fast420_packet) to nearly every routing/decode/choose_route/capability function, and threaded via `self.fastNNN_packet.as_deref()` triples at 7 call sites. This bloats signatures, invites arg-order mistakes, and forces every intermediate function to restate all three.
evidence: rg -c fast444_packet: lib.rs 81, compute.rs 81, viewport.rs 12, routing.rs 5. 7 occurrences of `self.fast444_packet.as_deref()` in lib.rs (each accompanied by 422/420 siblings). JpegMetalCapabilities::for_request (routing.rs:85) and choose_route (lib.rs:2703) both take all three explicitly.
reduction: Bundle the three options into one small enum/struct (a borrowed `FastPacketRef`/`FastPacketSet`, akin to the existing BatchedFastPacket) and pass that single value through the call graph.

title: Single-caller wrapper chains around batch decoders add indirection without value
description: Each batch decoder is fronted by a two-step wrapper chain: `_full_rgb_batch_to_surfaces` and `_full_rgb_batch_to_surfaces_into_output` both just forward to `_with_mode_and_output` supplying fast_batch_decode_mode() and None/Some(output). Each wrapper has exactly one caller.
evidence: try_decode_fast_subsampled_full_rgb_batch_to_surfaces (3272) and _into_output (3287) are 2-line forwarders to _with_mode_and_output (3303); rg confirms each wrapper has a single call site (7125/7131 and 6969/6975 respectively). Same pattern for fast444 (4356/4365) and region-scaled 420/422 wrappers (5875,6751).
reduction: Inline the wrappers into their single callers (pass mode/output directly), deleting ~10 forwarder functions.

title: RouteDecision/decision_error routing skeleton mirrors j2k-metal/routing.rs
description: routing.rs re-declares the RouteDecision enum (CpuHost/MetalKernel/RejectExplicitMetal/RejectUnsupportedBackend/MetalUnavailable), the ExplicitMetalRejection enum, profile_labels(), and decision_error() with the same shape and same j2k-metal-support helper calls as j2k-metal/routing.rs (and likely j2k-transcode-metal). Only the capability-computation (JpegMetalCapabilities) is genuinely JPEG-specific.
evidence: Both files define `pub(crate) enum RouteDecision` with matching variants and a `decision_error` mapping the reject/unavailable variants to errors; both call j2k_metal_support cpu_host_route/metal_kernel_route/etc. rg over crates/*/src/routing.rs shows the parallel item lists.
reduction: Hoist the RouteDecision enum + profile_labels + decision_error skeleton into j2k-metal-support (parameterized over a crate-specific error/rejection), leaving only the JPEG capability logic here.
verify_note: Verified both crates define pub(crate) enum RouteDecision with the same 5 variants (CpuHost, MetalKernel, RejectExplicitMetal{reason}, RejectUnsupportedBackend{request}, MetalUnavailable), both provide a decision_error mapping the reject/unavailable variants to errors, and both consume j2k_metal_support route-label helpers (cpu_host_route/metal_kernel_route/etc.). The parallel skeleton is real. But the est is slightly optimistic: the two differ in cfg strategy (jpeg uses cfg_attr allow(dead_code) so variants are always present, j2k uses #[cfg] to conditionally compile them), the RejectExplicitMetal payloads differ (MissingFastPacket/UnsupportedOutputFormat vs UnsupportedFormat{fmt}), profile_labels is an impl method here vs a free fn j2k_route_decision_profile there, and decision_error constructs crate-specific Error variants. Hoisting into j2k-metal-support requires making RouteDecision generic over the rejection type and a trait/closure to build each crate's Error, which adds generic boilerplate that offsets part of the ~50 LOC. Realistic net saving ~35.

title: 4-way region/scaled fan-out copy-pasted across plan builders and surface decoders
description: The four build_cuda_htj2k_grayscale_*_plan_with_profile methods are ~48 lines each and differ only in the DecodeSettings (optional target_resolution) and which native plan builder / from_grayscale_direct_plan[_region] variant they call; the report construction and report.emit("plan") block are byte-identical across all four. The same base/region/scaled/region_scaled fan-out is then repeated again in the decode_grayscale_cuda_resident_*_surface wrappers (each ~20 lines, differing only in the build_* call), and once more for the color variants.
evidence: Lines 412-424, 460-472, 507-519, 563-575 are identical CudaHtj2kProfileReport literals + report.emit("plan"). Lines 1194-1261 show four near-identical decode_grayscale_cuda_resident_*_surface bodies calling decode_grayscale_cuda_resident_surface_with_plan_profile with the same arg tuple. rg confirms the only differences are the request shape (Region/Scaled/RegionScaled).
reduction: Introduce a single request enum (Full/Region/Scaled/RegionScaled) or a small struct carrying roi/target_dims, and collapse the 4 plan builders into one parameterized helper plus 4 one-line wrappers; do the same for the grayscale/color surface decoders. The report literal and emit call become shared.
verify_note: Confirmed real, mergeable duplication. The four grayscale plan builders (decoder.rs 390-577) carry byte-identical CudaHtj2kProfileReport literals plus report.emit("plan") at 412-424/460-472/507-519/563-575; the only variation is DecodeSettings (default vs target_resolution), the native plan call (build_direct_grayscale_plan_with_context vs _region_with_context), and the CUDA flatten (from_grayscale_direct_plan vs _region) — all cleanly parameterizable by a Full/Region/Scaled/RegionScaled request. The grayscale surface decoders (1194-1261) and color surface decoders (1392-1454) are near-identical bodies differing only in which plan builder is invoked and the map-to-surface tail. Consolidation into one parameterized helper + thin wrappers preserves behavior and keeps the pub API. Est 160 is slightly optimistic once request-enum plumbing is added; ~140 is realistic.

title: direct_plan module fully public + re-exported but has zero non-test consumers
description: The eight direct_plan types (CudaHtj2kDecodePlan, CudaHtj2kCodeBlock, CudaHtj2kSubband, CudaHtj2kIdwtStep, CudaHtj2kStoreStep, CudaHtj2kRect, CudaHtj2kBandId, CudaHtj2kTransform) plus their 11 public accessors are re-exported from lib.rs, but no crate in the workspace outside j2k-cuda references them; internally they are used only by decoder.rs. Several accessors exist purely to serve integration tests.
evidence: rg -w across crates with --glob '!j2k-cuda/**' returns 0 external refs for all eight types. output_format(), output_origin(), and dispatch_count_hint() have zero callers anywhere except j2k-cuda/tests/htj2k_plan.rs (direct_plan.rs:372,377,453). The four build_cuda_htj2k_grayscale_*_plan_with_profile methods that surface these plans are only called from j2k-cuda/tests/htj2k_plan.rs and htj2k_cuda_kernels.rs.
reduction: Demote the direct_plan types and their accessors to pub(crate) (the plan struct is still needed internally by decoder.rs), drop the lib.rs re-export block, and move plan-shape assertions into #[cfg(test)] unit tests instead of exposing accessors for integration tests. Removes the re-export block and the test-only accessors (output_format/output_origin/dispatch_count_hint).

title: 18 attempts()/dispatches() counter getters on CudaEncodeStageAccelerator used only by unit tests
description: CudaEncodeStageAccelerator exposes 18 public zero-arg getters (deinterleave_attempts/dispatches, forward_rct/ict/dwt53/dwt97, quantize_subband, tier1_code_block, ht_code_block, packetization × attempts/dispatches). Each is a 3-line accessor over a private counter. None are called by production code or by any other crate; every call site is inside encode.rs's own #[cfg(test)] module.
evidence: rg for each getter shows all non-definition call sites are at encode.rs lines >=3540 (the test module starts at 3541); rg with --glob '!j2k-cuda/**' finds matches only in j2k-metal's own separate accelerator, not calls into j2k-cuda. No bench references (benches use .stats()/.execution() instead).
reduction: Expose a single pub fn dispatch_report()/attempts snapshot struct (already present internally) instead of 18 getters, or gate the getters behind #[cfg(test)]/pub(crate). Collapses ~88 lines of boilerplate to a handful.

title: codec.rs decode_tile_*_to_surface_impl: 4 near-identical CPU-fallback bodies
description: decode_tile_to_surface_impl, decode_tile_region_to_surface_impl, decode_tile_scaled_to_surface_impl, and decode_tile_region_scaled_to_surface_impl share the identical shape: validate_surface_request, if Cuda -> J2kDecoder::new + decode_*_to_device_with_session, else compute dims via DeviceDecodePlan, alloc `vec![0u8; stride*h]`, call CpuCodec::decode_tile_*, then wrap_surface. Only the plan request variant and the CpuCodec method differ.
evidence: Lines 88-97, 113-122, 140-149 are structurally identical (dims from DeviceDecodePlan::for_image(..., DeviceDecodeRequest::{Region|Scaled|RegionScaled}), stride, out vec, CpuCodec::decode_tile_*, wrap_surface). The 4 TileBatchDecodeSubmit impls (158-218) are likewise identical modulo the delegated impl.
reduction: Factor the CPU-fallback tail (dims->alloc->decode->wrap) into one helper taking a DeviceDecodeRequest and a decode closure; the four methods become thin dispatchers.
verify_note: Verified: codec.rs 54-149. The CPU-fallback tails at 88-97, 113-122, 140-149 are structurally identical (dims via DeviceDecodePlan::for_image(..., request).output_dims(), stride, out vec, CpuCodec::decode_tile_*, wrap_surface), differing only in the request variant and the decode fn/args — factorable into one helper taking a DeviceDecodeRequest and a decode closure, keeping the Cuda-branch dispatchers thin. However the reduction only applies to the CPU tail: the four TileBatchDecodeSubmit impls (158-218) have distinct trait signatures (roi/scale params) and cannot collapse. Net dedupable is ~6 lines per method minus the shared helper, so the est of 40 is inflated to ~25.

title: Combinatorial matrix of 10 public encode_lossless_from_cuda_buffer* wrappers
description: Ten public entry points cover single-vs-batch × host-bytes-vs-cuda-resident × with-report-vs-without × submit, most being one-line delegations (e.g. encode_* = submit_*()?.wait(); *_to_cuda_buffer = *_to_cuda_buffer_with_report()?.encoded). This is a wide surface where the non-report/non-resident variants add little over the *_with_report / submit forms.
evidence: encode_lossless_from_cuda_buffer (286) just calls submit(...).wait(); encode_lossless_from_cuda_buffer_to_cuda_buffer (323) just unwraps *_with_report. rg with --glob '!j2k-cuda/**' shows the only external callers are j2k-cuda/tests/encode_stage_api.rs; no production or sibling-crate consumers.
reduction: Keep the *_with_report and submit_* forms as the canonical API and drop or #[doc(hidden)] the convenience unwrap wrappers, or generate them via a macro; trims ~5 public functions.

title: region/scaled dispatch match-on-format repeated 4x in cuda-resident impls
description: decode_region/scaled/region_scaled_to_cuda_resident_surface_impl each repeat the same match fmt { Gray8|Gray16 => grayscale_*, Rgb8|Rgba8|Rgb16|Rgba16 => color_*, _ => Err(UnsupportedCudaRequest) } block, differing only in the grayscale/color helper called and its args.
evidence: Lines 1086-1096, 1115-1125, 1149-1170 are the same three-arm match with identical error arm using CUDA_HTJ2K_OUTPUT_FORMAT_UNSUPPORTED.
reduction: Extract a format-classifier (grayscale vs color vs unsupported) or dispatch via the request enum from finding #1 so the match exists once.
verify_note: Duplication is real but the '4x' count is overstated. Within 1072-1170 there are exactly three identical-shape three-arm matches (1086-1096 region, 1115-1125 scaled, 1149-1170 region_scaled), each with the same CUDA_HTJ2K_OUTPUT_FORMAT_UNSUPPORTED error arm. The '4th' match at 1011-1033 (full-frame) dispatches to *_with_profile variants with extra wall_started/collect_stage_timings args and a tuple return, so it is not the same shape; the batch match at 1056 is only 2-arm. A format-classifier (grayscale/color/unsupported) is a safe consolidation, but it mainly removes the repeated `_ => Err` arm and format-pattern lists (~3 lines/match) and is largely offset by the helper itself, so the safe net saving is small — est 25 is inflated to ~10.

title: dct53_1d module used only by its own tests/benches
description: The entire #[doc(hidden)] pub mod dct53_1d (Dwt53OneLevel, Dwt53Row, dct8_to_dwt53_float_linear, idct8_then_dwt53_float, dct8_blocks_to_dwt53_float_linear[_with_len], idct8_blocks_then_dwt53_float[_with_len], dct8_to_dwt53_reversible_i16, idct8_rounded_then_dwt53_reversible) is a standalone 1-D reference ladder that the real transcode pipeline never calls; dct53_2d.rs implements its own 2-D transform and does not import dct53_1d.
evidence: rg across crates/* shows every dct53_1d symbol referenced only in crates/j2k-transcode/src/dct53_1d.rs, crates/j2k-transcode/tests/dct53_1d.rs, and crates/j2k-transcode/benches/dct53.rs. `rg 'dct53_1d|dct8_to_dwt53|idct8_then|dct8_blocks|idct8_blocks' crates/j2k-transcode/src/dct53_2d.rs` returns nothing; no GPU crate (j2k-transcode-metal/cuda) references it. jpeg_to_htj2k.rs imports only from dct53_2d/dct97_2d/reversible53, never dct53_1d.
reduction: Delete src/dct53_1d.rs and its lib.rs declaration (and the dedicated tests/dct53_1d.rs + benches/dct53.rs 1-D cases), or if the 1-D reference is wanted keep it behind a test-only cfg instead of shipping it in the public crate.
verify_note: rg across crates/ and xtask confirms no non-test callers. The module is declared `#[doc(hidden)] pub mod dct53_1d` in crates/j2k-transcode/src/lib.rs:10. Every public symbol (dct8_to_dwt53_float_linear, idct8_then_dwt53_float, dct8_blocks_to_dwt53_float_linear[_with_len], idct8_blocks_then_dwt53_float[_with_len], dct8_to_dwt53_reversible_i16, idct8_rounded_then_dwt53_reversible, Dwt53OneLevel/Dwt53Row/Dct53RowLengthError) is referenced only inside src/dct53_1d.rs, tests/dct53_1d.rs, and benches/dct53.rs. The two bench_harness.rs hits are plain string literals ('dct53_1d_single_block_scalar'), not symbol uses. No GPU crate (metal/cuda), no build.rs, no macro/FFI, no other workspace crate references it. Being `#[doc(hidden)] pub` means it is exposed only so integration tests/benches can reach it, not real public API. Deleting src/dct53_1d.rs (332 LOC) plus the lib.rs line and the dedicated test/bench cases is safe. Estimate of 332 (the src file) is accurate.

title: dct53_multilevel module and dct53_2d oracle functions only reachable from tests/benches
description: dct53_multilevel (dct8x8_to_dwt53_multilevel_float_linear, idct8x8_then_dwt53_multilevel_float, Dwt53MultiLevel) is never called by the pipeline or GPU crates. It in turn is the only non-test caller of dct53_2d::dct8x8_to_dwt53_float_linear and ::idct8x8_then_dwt53_float; ::dct8x8_blocks_to_dwt53_float_linear has no non-test caller at all. So removing multilevel leaves three dct53_2d scalar functions with only test callers.
evidence: rg shows dct53_multilevel symbols only in src/dct53_multilevel.rs, benches/dct53.rs, tests/dct53_multilevel.rs, tests/bench_harness.rs. For dct53_2d: dct8x8_to_dwt53_float_linear and idct8x8_then_dwt53_float non-test callers are only src/dct53_multilevel.rs; dct8x8_blocks_to_dwt53_float_linear non-test callers = none (only dct53_2d self + benches/tests). Pipeline (jpeg_to_htj2k.rs:38-40) imports only dct8x8_blocks_then_dwt53_float, dct8x8_blocks_to_dwt53_float_linear_with_scratch, linearized_53_2d_from_plane.
reduction: Drop dct53_multilevel entirely and the three test-only dct53_2d functions (plus their tests/benches), keeping only the *_with_scratch and dct8x8_blocks_then_dwt53_float entry points the pipeline actually uses.
verify_note: dct53_multilevel is declared `#[doc(hidden)] pub mod` (lib.rs:13) and referenced outside its own file only in benches/dct53.rs, tests/dct53_multilevel.rs, and a bench_harness string literal — no pipeline/GPU/build/macro use. Verified the three dct53_2d oracle fns: `dct8x8_to_dwt53_float_linear` and `idct8x8_then_dwt53_float` have their sole non-test callers inside src/dct53_multilevel.rs (lines 93, 106); `dct8x8_blocks_to_dwt53_float_linear` has NO non-test callers at all (only self + tests/benches). The pipeline (jpeg_to_htj2k.rs) uses only the *_with_scratch and dct8x8_blocks_then_dwt53_float entry points, which are preserved. Removing dct53_multilevel would also drop the only callers of these three oracle fns, making them safe to delete together. Sizes: multilevel ~161 LOC; the three fns ~69 LOC (dct8x8_to_dwt53_float_linear ~42, dct8x8_blocks_to_dwt53_float_linear ~17, idct8x8_then_dwt53_float ~10), totaling ~230 rather than 250. Minor helpers like idct8x8_sample may also become dead, so 250 is a defensible ceiling; I set the tighter figure.

title: corpus_validation harness shipped in src/ but consumed only by one integration test
description: The #[doc(hidden)] pub mod corpus_validation (CorpusFixture, OwnedCorpusFixture, CorpusValidationOptions/Report/Error, validate_transcode_corpus, load_external_wsi_fixtures, 4 env-var consts) is external-WSI test scaffolding that inflates the shipped crate and its public surface but is only used by tests/corpus_validation.rs.
evidence: rg 'corpus_validation' across crates/* returns only src/corpus_validation.rs, lib.rs:8, and tests/corpus_validation.rs; no other crate imports it. All pub symbols resolve to that single test file.
reduction: Move the module into tests/ (a shared tests/common module) so it is compiled only for the test binary, removing 380 lines from the shipped library and its public API.

title: Integer-5/3 and float-9/7 batch pipelines are near-duplicate parallel code
description: The 5/3 and 9/7 batch paths maintain matched function pairs that differ only in concrete helper calls and a couple of struct fields: jpeg_tile_batch_to_htj2k_with_scratch vs jpeg_float97_tile_batch_to_htj2k_with_scratch (extract/collect/transform/encode/batch_output scaffolding ~90% identical), prepare_integer_batch_tile vs prepare_float97_batch_tile (~40 identical lines: extract, 1..=4 component check, component_sampling, decomposition_levels, all_unit_sampled, component_reports), batch_component_groups vs float97_batch_component_groups, same_batch_component_key vs same_float97_batch_component_key (differ by one field), store_integer_batch_wavelet vs store_float97_batch_wavelet, encode_integer_batch_tile vs encode_float97_batch_tile.
evidence: Direct read of the paired functions at the cited line numbers shows byte-for-byte identical preambles/scaffolding; e.g. lines 1508-1543 vs 1566-1601 differ only in DctExtractOptions and the extra preencoded/prequantized field inits; 1747-1762 vs 1764-1778 differ only by `left.component_index == right.component_index`.
reduction: Extract the shared batch-driver scaffolding and tile-preparation preamble into one generic helper parameterized over a small trait/closure (extract-options + per-component key + transform + encode), collapsing each pair into one implementation.
verify_note: Real duplication exists for several cited pairs, but the claim and estimate are overstated. Genuinely near-duplicate and mergeable via a trait/closure abstraction over the tile type: prepare_integer_batch_tile vs prepare_float97_batch_tile (1503/1561, differ only in DctExtractOptions::default vs dequantized_only and extra field inits), the two jpeg_*_tile_batch_to_htj2k_with_scratch drivers (1197/1305, differ in which prepare/transform/encode helpers are called and report fields), and batch_component_groups vs float97_batch_component_groups (1690/1719). However two caveats reduce mergeability/savings: (1) same_batch_component_key vs same_float97_batch_component_key (1747/1764) carry a genuine SEMANTIC difference — the integer key requires `left.component_index == right.component_index` while the float97 key does not, so naive consolidation would change float97 grouping behavior unless that predicate is parameterized. (2) store_integer_batch_wavelet vs store_float97_batch_wavelet (2416/2456) and especially encode_integer_batch_tile vs encode_float97_batch_tile (2783/2869) are NOT near-duplicate: the float97 store uses different DWT constructors/coefficient rounding and skips integer validation, and encode_float97_batch_tile has a large 3-way branch (preencoded_compact / preencoded / prequantized paths, flagged #[allow(clippy::too_many_lines)]) with no integer counterpart. Those two pairs share only report/timing scaffolding and cannot be cleanly collapsed. The mergeable pairs realistically net ~120-150 LOC after adding trait/generic boilerplate, not 300. Core defect is real, estimate inflated.

title: jpeg_to_htj2k.rs is a 5051-line god-file with five oversized functions
description: A single module holds the options types, error type, reports/timings, both batch pipelines, all accelerator-dispatch fallback ladders, and the scalar transform math. Five functions are individually large enough to require #[allow(clippy::too_many_lines)] suppressions (try_store_grouped_i16_preencoded_float97_batches, try_store_prequantized_float97_batch_group, encode_float97_precomputed_tiles_batch, encode_float97_batch_tile, jpeg_to_htj2k_with_scratch), and one more carries too_many_arguments.
evidence: wc -l = 5051; `rg '#\[allow\(clippy::too_many_lines' jpeg_to_htj2k.rs` returns 5 hits plus struct_excessive_bools and too_many_arguments. Public API is a thin re-export list in lib.rs:28-35 while the module mixes ~30 private helper fns.
reduction: Split into submodules (options/report types, integer path, float97 path, accelerator-dispatch, scalar math) and decompose the flagged functions; removes the allow() suppressions and shrinks review surface.

title: Hand-written profile-field emission repeats field->string mapping ~63 times
description: Seven push_* helpers each list ("field_name", value.to_string()) tuples one per TranscodeTimingReport counter, ~63 .to_string() lines of mechanical boilerplate, while the same struct already uses a saturating_add_fields! macro (lines 957-963) to avoid exactly this kind of per-field repetition in AddAssign.
evidence: `sed -n '534,816p' | grep -c to_string()` = 63; add_assign at 956-1013 demonstrates the macro pattern already exists in the file but is not applied to field emission.
reduction: Introduce a field!(name) => (stringify!(name), self.name.to_string()) style macro (mirroring saturating_add_fields!) to fold the push_* bodies to one line per field.

title: Four public decode_tiles_* batch fns duplicate scoped-thread orchestration
description: The four public batch entry points share a near-identical body: empty-jobs early return, worker_count/chunk_size computation, std::thread::scope spawn loop, join-with-resume_unwind loop, and collect_indexed_batch_results. Only the per-chunk decode function called differs (decode_tile_job_chunk vs region/scaled variants). The four *_job_chunk helpers (289-389) likewise repeat the same DecoderContext + parallelism + J2kScratchPool setup.
evidence: Lines 110-147, 151-187, 191-227, 231-275 are byte-for-byte identical except the chunk fn name; decode_tile_job_chunk (289), decode_tile_region_job_chunk (308), decode_tile_scaled_job_chunk (328) share identical ctx/pool setup differing only in the inner *_into_in_context call.
reduction: Extract a generic helper fn run_tile_batch<J>(jobs, options, chunk_fn) that owns the scope/spawn/join/collect boilerplate and takes a closure/fn pointer for the per-chunk work; collapse the four publics to one-line wrappers. ~90-110 LOC.
verify_note: Confirmed the three fns at batch.rs:110/151/191 are near-identical, differing only in the chunk fn name and job type. The 'byte-for-byte identical' claim is overstated for the fourth (231-275): it additionally builds shared_direct_plan via build_repeated_direct_color_region_plan and threads it into the chunk fn. Nonetheless all four share the empty-check + worker_count/chunk_size + thread::scope/spawn/join/resume_unwind/collect_indexed_batch_results boilerplate and can be safely folded into a generic run_tile_batch helper taking a per-chunk closure (the fourth's closure captures shared_direct_plan) with no behavior/perf change. Genuine and safe; estimate marginally high given the fourth's extra setup remains.

title: Roundtrip-validation family repeats decode + error-mapping boilerplate
description: validate_lossless_roundtrip, _component_roundtrip, _high_bit_component_roundtrip, _typed_component_roundtrip and _high_bit_typed_component_roundtrip each repeat the same shape: External early-return, Image::new(...).map_err(...), decode via context, geometry check, per-plane metadata check, per-sample compare. The closure |err| J2kError::Backend(format!("encoded codestream validation failed: {err}")) is written 15 times. validate_full_resolution_component_samples vs _typed_component_samples are ~identical except how expected_samples is derived.
evidence: rg -c 'encoded codestream validation failed' crates/j2k/src/encode.rs = 15. The two full_resolution_*_samples fns (2720-2757, 2759-2790) differ only in expected_samples derivation and the error-message string; the per-sample loop is identical.
reduction: Introduce a helper closure/fn for the map_err (fn validation_backend_err(err) -> J2kError) and a shared per-plane/per-sample comparison routine parameterized by expected accessor; unify the two full_resolution samples fns. ~70-100 LOC.
verify_note: Real duplication exists but the estimate is heavily inflated. The map_err 'encoded codestream validation failed' pattern occurs 15x, but each is already a one-line closure; replacing with a fn reference saves ~0 LOC (adds ~3 for the helper) — readability only. The five validate_lossless_*_roundtrip fns are materially different (decode_native vs decode_components_with_context vs decode_native_components; inline data comparison vs helper delegation; high-bit variants do metadata-only checks) and are not trivially mergeable. Only the two validate_full_resolution_*_samples fns (2720-2757, 2759-2790) are genuinely unifiable — identical per-sample loop differing only in expected_samples derivation, bit_depth/signed accessor, and label — safely parameterizable. Realistic saving is ~15-20 LOC, not 80.

title: encode.rs is a 2930-line god-file mixing options, samples, entry points, validation, PSNR math
description: encode.rs holds ~15 public option/sample/report types, all lossless/lossy/component/typed encode entry points, backend resolution, native-option builders, decomposition-level policy, RequiredEncodeStages bitset, roundtrip validation, and PSNR/bit-depth math (sample_value, read_le_sample_value, sign_extend_sample, bits_per_pixel, usize_to_f64). This concentration makes review and reuse hard and is the crate's single largest file.
evidence: wc -l = 2930; function outline shows distinct concern clusters: option types (27-485), sample types+geometry (487-875), encode entries (946-1244), routing/native-options (1244-1885), decomposition policy (1898-1990), stage bitset (1991-2141), validation+psnr (2249-2790).
reduction: Split into encode/options.rs (option+sample types), encode/entry.rs (public fns), encode/validate.rs (roundtrip + psnr helpers), encode/policy.rs (decomposition levels + RequiredEncodeStages). Improves reviewability; ~0 net LOC deleted but enables the duplication cleanups above.

title: decode_rows_u8_bounded / decode_rows_u16_bounded duplicate stripe loop
description: The two bounded row-decode methods share the entire stripe-iteration scaffold (layout compute, pool alloc, while-y loop, stripe_len checked_mul with identical SizeOverflow error, decode_region_into_cached, y advance, final DecodeOutcome). They diverge only in the per-row emit (u8 raw copy vs u16 LE unpack).
evidence: Lines 544-579 vs 597-635 are identical except the inner write block (568-572 vs 621-629) and the u16 samples_per_row fetch.
reduction: Extract a stripe-iteration helper taking a per-row-emit closure, or a generic over sample type, so the loop scaffold exists once. ~25-30 LOC.

title: Re-exported types with no external consumers widen the public surface
description: Several decode types are re-exported at crate root but are only ever used internally as field/return types of J2kDecodedComponents/J2kDecodedNativeComponents. Exporting the names as top-level public API commits the crate to their stability without any consumer needing them by name.
evidence: rg for J2kDecodedColorSpace and J2kNativeComponentPlane across crates finds references only inside crates/j2k/src/decode.rs (as struct fields/methods) and the lib.rs re-export line; 0 references in any other crate, test, or bench.
reduction: Drop these two from the crate-root pub use (keep them pub within the decode module if reachable via the returned structs), narrowing the committed public surface. Minimal LOC but reduces API commitment.

title: Infrastructure helpers copy-pasted verbatim between the two large bins instead of living in lib.rs
description: Roughly two dozen helper fns are defined identically (or with only an error-string prefix difference) in both giant binaries: env_truthy/env_falsey; the BatchSizeConfig + batch_size_config_from_env/_from_values + legacy_batch_sizes_from_env + combined_batch_sizes + parse_batch_sizes block; the manifest path block canonicalize_manifest_row_path/manifest_relocation_candidates/normal_path_suffixes/join_path_labels; the manifest-field block manifest_column/optional_manifest_column/manifest_field/manifest_required_value/manifest_optional_value/validate_manifest_value; git_revision(_label)/git_dirty_status(_label)/build_profile_label; host_hardware_label + 3 cfg'd host_hardware_from_platform; command_stdout; fnv1a64_hex/fnv1a64_hex_slices; join_usizes/join_string_labels/mib_per_second/dimensions_label; is_publishable_license_status/default_case_batch_sizes_present/default_mixed_batch_sizes_present; mixed_external_max/min_distinct_inputs; external_input_dirs/sanitized_stem/external_corpus_category/external_source_label. The crate already ships a library (lib.rs) that is the natural home for these; today it only holds ~6 helpers.
evidence: rg 'fn <name>' matched in BOTH files for 28 helper names (env_truthy, env_falsey, combined_batch_sizes, parse_batch_sizes, canonicalize_manifest_row_path, manifest_relocation_candidates, normal_path_suffixes, join_path_labels, manifest_column, optional_manifest_column, manifest_field, manifest_required_value, manifest_optional_value, validate_manifest_value, git_revision, git_dirty_status, build_profile_label, command_stdout, fnv1a64_hex, fnv1a64_hex_slices, join_usizes, join_string_labels, mib_per_second, is_publishable_license_status, default_case_batch_sizes_present, default_mixed_batch_sizes_present, mixed_external_max_distinct_inputs, mixed_external_min_distinct_inputs). Direct read confirmed byte-identical bodies for fnv1a64_hex_slices, env_truthy/env_falsey, command_stdout, git_dirty_status/build_profile_label/host_hardware_from_platform, and the entire manifest path/field blocks (only literal 'encode manifest' vs 'fixture manifest' differs).
reduction: Move the shared helpers into crates/j2k-compare/src/lib.rs (parameterize the manifest error-prefix), delete both copies from the bins, and import them. Removes one full copy (~450-500 lines) plus shrinks the two god-files.
verify_note: rg confirmed all 28 named helpers exist exactly once in BOTH jp2k_fixture_compare.rs and jp2k_encode_compare.rs. Diffing extracted function bodies showed byte-identical bodies for fnv1a64_hex_slices, env_truthy, env_falsey, command_stdout, git_dirty_status, build_profile_label, mib_per_second, parse_batch_sizes, canonicalize_manifest_row_path, manifest_relocation_candidates; the only divergences are manifest_field and validate_manifest_value which differ ONLY in the literal 'fixture manifest' vs 'encode manifest' error prefix (exactly as claimed, parameterizable). Helpers are self-contained (operate on &str/&[u8]/Path/Vec<usize>, no bin-local types), so moving to lib.rs is sound. However est is inflated: summed body lines for all 28 helpers in one copy = 287, with zero preceding doc/attribute lines; net removal after moving one canonical copy to lib is ~287-315, not 450-500.

title: Whole 'case vs mixed' pipeline duplicated inside each big bin
description: fixture_compare implements every stage twice - once for per-case batches and once for 'mixed' batches: measure_case_batch_rows/measure_mixed_batch_rows, decode_batch/decode_mixed_batch, decode_j2k_batch/decode_j2k_mixed_batch, decode_external_batch/decode_external_mixed_batch, measurement_row/mixed_measurement_row, skip_row/mixed_skip_row. decode_j2k_batch (2745) and decode_j2k_mixed_batch (2818) are structurally identical 4-arm match blocks differing only in how each job's input/stride is sourced (BatchInputs vs mixed_case_at). encode_compare mirrors this with measure_case_rows/measure_mixed_rows, measure_case_states/measure_mixed_states, measure_case_encoder_once/measure_mixed_encoder_once, measurement_row/mixed_measurement_row, skip_row/mixed_skip_row.
evidence: Read of jp2k_fixture_compare.rs:2745-2918 shows decode_j2k_batch and decode_j2k_mixed_batch as the same Operation Full/Region/Scaled/RegionScaled match, only the job-input closure differs. Item listing shows the systematic _batch/_mixed_batch and case/mixed fn-name pairing across both bins.
reduction: Introduce a small trait/closure that yields (input, stride, operation) per index so a single generic decode/measure/row routine serves both case and mixed paths; collapse each pair into one function.

title: Two multi-thousand-line single-file binaries acting as god-files
location: crates/j2k-compare/src/bin/jp2k_fixture_compare.rs (3878 LOC, ~135 free fns), crates/j2k-compare/src/bin/jp2k_encode_compare.rs (2840 LOC, ~130 free fns)
description: Each benchmark is one flat file mixing manifest parsing, corpus discovery, codec probing (openjph/kakadu/grok/openjpeg), PNM IO, publication-gate policy, metadata emission, and the timing loop. This is what forces the cross-file copy-paste in the other findings and is why #![allow(too_many_lines)] is set crate-wide. Extracting the shared infra (finding 1) into lib.rs plus splitting each bin into modules (manifest, discovery, comparators, emit, measure) would make both far smaller and reviewable.
evidence: wc -l: fixture 3878, encode 2840. Cargo.toml sets [lints.clippy] too_many_lines = "allow". Function inventory via rg shows 130+ top-level fns per file.
reduction: After hoisting shared helpers to lib.rs, break each bin into submodules (or a bin + supporting lib modules); drop the blanket too_many_lines allow.

title: Oversized policy functions hidden behind crate-wide too_many_lines allow
location: crates/j2k-compare/src/bin/jp2k_fixture_compare.rs:1950 publication_blockers (~217 lines), :1507 emit_metadata (~150), :695 fixture_cases (~140); crates/j2k-compare/src/bin/jp2k_encode_compare.rs:1803 publication_blockers (~157), :1678 emit_metadata (~125)
description: publication_blockers and emit_metadata are long, deeply-branched functions that concatenate many independent gate checks / metadata rows. They are the main beneficiaries of the too_many_lines=allow lint suppression and are hard to review or test in isolation. The two publication_blockers implementations also share a common shape (min-count gates, license gate, mixed-group gates) that could be factored into reusable gate helpers.
evidence: Function start lines from rg item inventory; spans computed from adjacent fn starts (publication_blockers 1950->2167 in fixture). Cargo.toml sets too_many_lines = "allow".
reduction: Split each into per-concern helpers (e.g. one fn per gate, one fn per metadata section) and share the common gate helpers across both bins; then the allow can be narrowed or removed.

title: external_corpus_category needle tables diverge between bins
description: Both define external_corpus_category with the same (needle -> category) lookup loop but maintain separate, drifting keyword lists (fixture has iso/conformance/jpylyzer/tecnick/testimages/tcga/openslide/space/gdal entries the encode copy lacks). This is duplicate logic with silent divergence in corpus classification.
evidence: Read of both bodies: identical loop structure and 'external-unspecified' fallback; fixture table has ~26 entries, encode table has ~13, overlapping subset.
reduction: Move external_corpus_category (and sanitized_stem, external_source_label) to lib.rs with a single canonical needle table shared by both bins.
verify_note: Read of both bodies confirms: external_corpus_category has identical loop structure and 'external-unspecified' fallback in both; fixture table has 25 needle/category entries, encode table has 13 (a reordered overlapping subset) -- genuine accidental drift. sanitized_stem is byte-identical in both. external_source_label differs only in error message text ('external fixture path...cannot be represented safely' vs 'external image path...'). All three are self-contained (Path -> String) and safely movable to lib.rs with a single canonical needle table. Note: consolidating gives the encode bin fixture's larger category set, a minor behavior harmonization affecting only benchmark metadata labels (not correctness), which is the intent. est ~40 is accurate (removing one copy each: ~22 + ~14 + ~9 lines).

title: Five fully-unused pub helper/fixture functions
description: These pub functions are exported via lib.rs re-exports but never called anywhere in the workspace (nor internally). centered_rect and cuda_bench_required have zero references at all. jpeg_grayscale_8x8_gray/jpeg_baseline_420_16x16_rgb are dead .to_vec() wrappers whose underlying consts (JPEG_GRAYSCALE_8X8_GRAY / JPEG_BASELINE_420_16X16_RGB) are the ones consumers actually import. lossless_rgb_16bit_422_3x3_jpeg has no caller in tests or src.
evidence: rg -l -w over crates/* excluding this crate returns 0 files for each; internal rg in crates/j2k-test-support/src shows only the definition + the lib.rs re-export line (centered_rect, cuda_bench_required, jpeg_grayscale_8x8_gray, jpeg_baseline_420_16x16_rgb). lossless_rgb_16bit_422_3x3_jpeg: `rg -n -w lossless_rgb_16bit_422_3x3_jpeg crates` shows only its own definition. Note JPEG_GRAYSCALE_8X8_GRAY has 1 external user and JPEG_BASELINE_420_16X16_RGB has 1 external user, so consumers use the consts, not these wrappers.
reduction: Delete the five functions and their lib.rs re-export entries.
verify_note: All five functions are referenced ONLY at their definition site plus the lib.rs re-export (centered_rect: pixels.rs:22 + lib.rs:46; cuda_bench_required: cuda.rs:21 + lib.rs:20; jpeg_grayscale_8x8_gray: fixtures.rs:41 + lib.rs:27; jpeg_baseline_420_16x16_rgb: fixtures.rs:31 + lib.rs:24; lossless_rgb_16bit_422_3x3_jpeg: jpeg_fixtures.rs:939, re-exported via `pub use jpeg_fixtures::*`). rg -w across all of crates/ and xtask found no callers in any src/tests/benches/examples. The crate Cargo.toml has publish=false, so it is a workspace-internal test-support crate — unreferenced pub items are not a real public API and are genuinely dead within the workspace. The finding correctly notes the underlying consts JPEG_GRAYSCALE_8X8_GRAY and JPEG_BASELINE_420_16X16_RGB DO have external users, but the wrapper functions do not. No macro/stringify/glob use hides a caller. est_loc_saved of 30 is slightly high; the five bodies total ~28 lines plus a few partial re-export tokens.

title: Internal-only helpers and consts leaked through the public API
location: crates/j2k-test-support/src/metal_shader.rs:4,9 (metal_kernel_names, host_compiles_metal_pipeline); src/jpeg_fixtures.rs:820,825 (LOSSLESS_YCBCR_3X3_PIXELS, LOSSLESS_YCBCR_16BIT_3X3_PIXELS); src/fixtures.rs:14,18,22 (JPEG_BASELINE_444_8X8_RGB, JPEG_BASELINE_422_16X8_RGB, JPEG_BASELINE_420_RESTART_32X16_RGB)
description: These symbols are pub (and, for the metal helpers, re-exported from lib.rs) but are only used inside this crate. metal_kernel_names/host_compiles_metal_pipeline are only called by unwired_metal_kernels (the one metal helper actually consumed externally). The two LOSSLESS_YCBCR pixel consts and the three JPEG_*_RGB reference-pixel consts are only referenced by sibling fixture fns in the same module; no consumer imports them.
evidence: rg external count = 0 for all six; internal rg shows metal_kernel_names/host_compiles_metal_pipeline used only at metal_shader.rs:24/33; LOSSLESS_YCBCR consts used only at jpeg_fixtures.rs:1120/1125/1129 and 1134/1314/1318; JPEG_*_RGB consts used only by their own *_rgb() fns in fixtures.rs. External callers of unwired_metal_kernels: j2k-{metal,jpeg-metal,transcode-metal}/tests/shader_integrity.rs.
reduction: Drop pub on the two metal helpers and the five consts (make them private / pub(crate)) and remove metal_kernel_names/host_compiles_metal_pipeline from the lib.rs re-export, shrinking the public surface.

title: fnv1a64_hex re-implemented 7x in crates that already depend on this crate
description: j2k-test-support already exports fnv1a64_hex, and both j2k-cuda and j2k-compare list j2k-test-support as a (dev/normal) dependency. Yet five files (plus j2k-compare adds a fnv1a64_hex_slices wrapper twice) copy the exact same 7-line FNV-1a implementation instead of importing it.
evidence: `rg -n 'fn fnv1a64' crates` shows the copies; the j2k-cuda body is byte-identical to lib.rs:283-289. crates/j2k-compare/Cargo.toml:15 has j2k-test-support under [dependencies]; crates/j2k-cuda/Cargo.toml:57 has it as a dev/feature dep.
