Many tests are large resident-dispatch assertions (130-168 lines each).
evidence: Last #[cfg(test)] at lib.rs:2841 -> ~4511 trailing lines; cuda/encode.rs:3540 -> ~1814; native encode.rs:8513 -> ~2302. Big test fns e.g. lib.rs:4425 assert_table_mixed_region_scaled_buffer_groups_resident(168), rgb8_table_mixed_* dispatch tests 130-163 lines.
reduction: Move these inline test modules to tests/ integration files or a sibling foo_tests.rs included via 'mod tests;' (as metal/compute.rs already does with compute/tests.rs). Extract repeated resident-dispatch assertion boilerplate into shared test helpers to cut the 130-168-line near-identical test bodies.

title: Near-duplicate owned vs compact_owned and 12-bit region decode bodies
description: encode_preencoded_htj2k_97_owned_with_accelerator (144 lines) and encode_preencoded_htj2k_97_compact_owned_with_accelerator (156 lines) share identical validation prologues and structure, differing only in the image/component type (PreencodedHtj2k97Image vs ...CompactImage). Similarly decode_progressive12_region_into (139) and decode_extended12_region_into (148) are parallel 12-bit region paths.
evidence: encode.rs:1955-1959 and 2099-2103 show byte-identical guard blocks ('invalid dimensions'; components.is_empty()||len>MAX_J2K_SPEC_COMPONENTS). decoder.rs:3397 decode_progressive12_region_into vs :3797 decode_extended12_region_into are sibling 12-bit region cores of near-equal length.
reduction: Introduce a trait (e.g. PreencodedImageView) over the owned/compact component storage and share one generic body; likewise factor the common 12-bit region setup/output-write into a shared helper parameterized by the scan-decode step.
verify_note: The byte-identical guard blocks are real (encode.rs 1955-1961 vs 2099-2105: 'invalid dimensions' + component-count check identical). encode_preencoded_htj2k_97_owned (1950-2093) and _compact_owned (2094-2249) are near line-for-line parallel dispatchers differing only in compact_* helper variants, the compact image destructure, and one extra marker-option guard — genuinely mergeable behind a PreencodedImageView-style trait (~100 net). The decoder pair (decode_progressive12_region_into @3397 / decode_extended12_region_into @3797) are parallel dispatchers (both branch on output/color_space to color444/subsampled/four_component region cores), but the underlying cores are fundamentally different (progressive multi-scan plan vs sequential extended plan) and cannot merge — only the dispatcher shell (~80) is shareable. So the reduction is real but the 200 estimate is somewhat optimistic; ~160 net is defensible.

title: Repeated forwarder triplets (bare / _into_output / _with_output) across jpeg-metal decode variants
description: Nearly every batch decode variant is expressed as three near-identical functions: a bare version calling _with_output(...,None), an _into_output version calling _with_output(...,Some(output)), and the real _with_output body. This boilerplate is copy-pasted for 420/422/444 x rgb x full/region-scaled.
evidence: compute.rs:4356/4365/4380 (fast444_full_rgb: bare, _into_output, _with_output) and :6751/6762/6776 (fast422_region_scaled_rgb) show the identical 3-fn shape; the pattern recurs for each format/op combination among the 43 try_decode_fast* fns.
reduction: Replace the bare/_into_output pair with a single public fn taking Option<&MetalBatchOutputBuffer> (callers already have the Option at hand), or generate them with a small macro. Removes ~2 wrappers per variant (~20+ fns).
verify_note: The triplet pattern is real and trivially mergeable: bare -> with_output(None) and _into_output -> with_output(Some) are pure forwarders (verified at 4356/4365/4380 and 6751/6762/6776). However the scope is heavily overstated: rg finds only 4 *_into_output forwarders and 4 bare *_batch_to_surfaces forwarders (8 wrappers, ~88 LOC), not the claimed '~20+ fns' — the bulk of the 43 try_decode_fast* fns are single-tile to_surface variants that lack the triplet. Collapsing the 8 wrappers into the 4 Option-taking with_output fns (updating 4 into_output call sites) nets roughly 70 LOC, not 180. Reduction is safe/sound but est is ~2.5x inflated.

title: Oversized encode orchestrators in j2k-native encode.rs (encode_impl, multitile, layered)
description: encode_impl is 543 lines and encode_multitile_impl 240; encode_prepared_resolution_packets_layered 243 and prepare_subband 158. These conflate validation, tiling, DWT/quantize/tier1 stage orchestration, and codestream assembly inline, making the single-tile vs multitile paths hard to keep in sync.
evidence: awk fn-span: encode_impl 543 @3426, encode_typed_component_planes_53_i64 282 @578, encode_typed_component_planes_53_i64_multitile 267 @860, encode_multitile_impl 240 @4540, encode_prepared_resolution_packets_layered 243 @7124, prepare_subband 158 @6719.
reduction: Extract per-stage helpers (validate_options, build_tiles, encode_tile_stages, assemble_codestream) shared by single- and multi-tile impls so both paths call the same stage functions; target <150 lines per orchestrator.

title: Production-visible *_for_test helper functions embedded in j2k-metal/compute.rs
description: Large test-only helpers live in the production module rather than behind cfg(test) or in the compute/tests.rs module, including a 753-line pack_classic_split_mq_raw_tokens_for_test and a 457-line encode_classic_tier1_code_blocks_via_split_mq_raw_tokens_gpu_pack_for_test_with_emit_route. They inflate the monolith and ship in non-test builds.
evidence: rg 'fn .*_for_test': compute.rs:652 with_isolated_runtime_for_device_for_test, :13307 pack_classic_split_mq_raw_tokens_for_test (753-line span); awk lists encode_classic_tier1_..._for_test_with_emit_route 457 @12788 and execute_flattened_hybrid_cpu_tier1_direct_color_plan_batch_for_test @3258.
reduction: Move these helpers into compute/tests.rs (already the 'mod tests;' at 17750) or gate them with #[cfg(any(test, feature="test-util"))] so they are excluded from release builds and removed from the production file's line count.

title: Redundant no-op cuda-oxide-* feature passthroughs on adapter crates
description: j2k-cuda declares 6 individual features (cuda-oxide-j2k-encode, -j2k-decode-store, -j2k-dequantize, -j2k-idwt, -htj2k-decode, -htj2k-encode) and j2k-transcode-cuda declares 3 (cuda-oxide-j2k-encode, -htj2k-encode, -transcode). Each is defined as ["cuda-runtime", "j2k-cuda-runtime/<same>"], but the crate's own cuda-runtime feature already enables ALL of those runtime sub-features unconditionally. Enabling an individual feature therefore produces exactly the same build as cuda-runtime, so no reduced/subset build is ever achievable through them.
evidence: j2k-cuda cuda-runtime = [...all 7 cuda-oxide-* including copy-u8...]; individual features each imply cuda-runtime. `rg -c 'feature="cuda-oxide' crates/j2k-cuda/src` and crates/j2k-transcode-cuda/src both return 0 cfg sites — these features gate nothing in the adapter crate; all 333 cuda-oxide-* cfg sites live only in j2k-cuda-runtime.
reduction: Delete the 9 individual cuda-oxide-* passthrough features from j2k-cuda and j2k-transcode-cuda; keep only cuda-runtime and cuda-profiling. If per-kernel subset builds are actually wanted, make cuda-runtime NOT auto-enable the sub-features and let individual features add them a la carte.
verify_note: Verified: rg finds 0 cfg sites keyed on cuda-oxide-* in crates/j2k-cuda/src and crates/j2k-transcode-cuda/src; all 333 cuda-oxide cfg sites live in j2k-cuda-runtime/src. In j2k-cuda, cuda-runtime bundles all 7 j2k-cuda-runtime/cuda-oxide-* sub-features, and each individual passthrough feature also implies cuda-runtime, so enabling any individual feature is exactly equivalent to enabling cuda-runtime (no subset selection is possible) and gates nothing locally. No other crate consumes j2k-cuda/cuda-oxide-* or j2k-transcode-cuda/cuda-oxide-* (rg over all Cargo.toml shows only self-referential defs), so deleting the 6 (j2k-cuda: lines 34-45) + 3 (transcode-cuda: lines 30-32) = 9 passthroughs is safe. Estimate is slightly inflated: the deletable lines total ~15, not 20.

title: j2k-native disables all clippy::pedantic while every other crate warns
description: The largest engine crate sets [lints.clippy] all={level=warn} and pedantic="allow", uniquely opting the entire pedantic group OFF. Every other crate (and the workspace default) uses pedantic={level=warn, priority=-1} with targeted allows. This means the most complex, security-relevant codec crate receives the least static-analysis coverage, and its lint posture diverges from the rest of the workspace with no documented rationale.
evidence: crates/j2k-native/Cargo.toml lines 53-55: `all = { level = "warn", priority = -1 }` then `pedantic = "allow"`. All other [lints.clippy] tables set pedantic=warn.
reduction: Promote j2k-native to pedantic=warn with an explicit, documented allow list matching its actual needs (SIMD casts etc.), aligning it with the workspace convention instead of a blanket opt-out.

title: Unconditional #[allow(dead_code)] hides a truly unused pub(crate) encoder fn
description: encode_code_block_with_style is annotated with an unconditional #[allow(dead_code)] (not cfg-tied like the CUDA sites) and has zero callers anywhere in the workspace — only its own definition matches. It is pub(crate), so no external consumer can reach it. This is genuinely removable dead code that the allow attribute masks, in a forbid-unsafe safe crate where dead-code checks would otherwise catch it.
evidence: `rg 'encode_code_block_with_style\b' | grep -v with_style_i64` returns only the definition line 328. Contrast with the 57 other dead_code allows which are cfg_attr(not(feature=...)) gated and legitimate for feature-off builds.
reduction: Delete encode_code_block_with_style (and the i32->i64 wrapper it uniquely calls if that becomes unused); remove the #[allow(dead_code)].
verify_note: Verified: crates/j2k-native/src/j2c/bitplane_encode.rs line 328 defines pub(crate) fn encode_code_block_with_style (i32 wrapper) under an unconditional #[allow(dead_code)] (line 327). rg across all crates/xtask (including tests/benches) finds only the definition — no caller anywhere; line 287 and 521 call encode_code_block_with_style_i64 (the i64 variant), not this wrapper. It is pub(crate), so not part of any public API. Safe to delete along with the allow. However the finding overstates the helper cleanup: i32_coefficients_to_i64 is NOT uniquely called by this fn — it is also used at lines 276 and 501 — so it stays. Actual deletable span is ~19 lines (327-345), not 25.

title: unsafe-usage count inflated ~35% by GPU kernel sources, not host FFI
description: Of 614 total 'unsafe' occurrences, ~160 live in the 10 embedded SIMT GPU kernel source trees (cuda_oxide_*/simt/src/main.rs) that are compiled to PTX via build.rs from Cargo.toml.in templates — they are not workspace members and never link into the host Rust binary. Two of them additionally carry #![allow(static_mut_refs)]. Reasoning about the workspace's real host-side unsafe surface (~454) should exclude these; they also mean audit lints like unsafe_op_in_unsafe_fn do not apply to a large chunk of the counted unsafe.
evidence: `rg unsafe crates/j2k-cuda-runtime/src/cuda_oxide_*/simt` = 160; build.rs references src/cuda_oxide_*/simt/src/main.rs + Cargo.toml.in as rerun-if-changed; no Cargo.toml under any src/ dir (not workspace members). static_mut_refs allow only in transcode/j2k_idwt simt main.rs.
reduction: Move the simt kernel crates out of src/ (e.g. kernels/ or a dedicated non-member path) so unsafe/lint metrics reflect host code, and document that kernel sources are governed separately; at minimum exclude them from workspace unsafe accounting.

title: 164 #[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)] signal a systemic wide-parameter API smell
description: too_many_arguments is by far the most common allow (164 of 267). Rather than suppressing per call site, this many occurrences indicates the codec/transcode hot-path functions and core traits carry long positional parameter lists, which is a maintainability and correctness hazard (easy to transpose args of the same type). This is the largest #[allow] category and represents deferred structural debt, not a style nit.
evidence: allow-lint breakdown: 164 too_many_arguments, next 59 dead_code, 48 similar_names. Spread across j2k-cuda encode/decode/codec, transcode dct/dwt, native block encoders, and j2k-core traits.
reduction: Introduce param/context structs (e.g. an EncodeParams/BlockCtx) for the worst offenders in encode.rs/decoder.rs/jpeg_to_htj2k.rs and the core traits, removing the allow at those sites; this also improves call-site readability and prevents same-type arg transposition bugs.
